117	
118	class SamplingParams(
119	        msgspec.Struct,
120	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
121	        # required for @cached_property.
122	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
123	    """Sampling parameters for text generation.
124	
125	    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
126	    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
127	    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
131	        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
132	            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
133	            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
134	            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
135	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
136	            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
137	            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
138	            tokens.
139	        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
140	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
141	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
142	            repeat tokens.
143	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
144	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
145	            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
146	            the model to repeat tokens.
147	        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
148	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
149	            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
150	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
151	            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
152	        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
153	            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
154	        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
155	            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
156	            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
157	        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
158	        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
159	            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
160	        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
161	            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
162	            the stop tokens are special tokens.
163	        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
164	            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
165	            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
166	            can complete the sequence.
167	        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
168	            output text. Defaults to False.
169	        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
170	            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
171	        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
172	        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
173	            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
174	        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
175	            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
176	            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
177	            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
178	            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
179	            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
180	            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
181	        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
182	        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
183	        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
184	        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
185	            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
186	        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
187	            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
188	            a first argument.
189	        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
190	            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
191	            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
192	            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
193	        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
194	            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
195	        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
196	            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
197	        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
198	            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
199	            Defaults to None.
200	        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
201	            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
202	            sampling implementations.
203	    """
204	
205	    n: int = 1
206	    best_of: Optional[int] = None
207	    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
208	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
209	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
210	    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
211	    temperature: float = 1.0
212	    top_p: float = 1.0
213	    top_k: int = 0
214	    min_p: float = 0.0
215	    seed: Optional[int] = None
216	    stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
217	    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
218	    ignore_eos: bool = False
219	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16
220	    min_tokens: int = 0
221	    logprobs: Optional[int] = None
222	    prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
223	    # NOTE: This parameter is only exposed at the engine level for now.
224	    # It is not exposed in the OpenAI API server, as the OpenAI API does
225	    # not support returning only a list of token IDs.
226	    detokenize: bool = True
227	    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
228	    spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True
229	    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type. We use Any here because
230	    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type is not supported by msgspec.
231	    logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
232	    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
233	    truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
234	    output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE
235	
236	    # The below fields are not supposed to be used as an input.
237	    # They are set in post_init.
238	    output_text_buffer_length: int = 0
239	    _all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)
240	
241	    # Fields used to construct logits processors
242	    guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None
243	    logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
244	    allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
245	    extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None
246	
247	    # Fields used for bad words
248	    bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None
249	    _bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None
250	
251	    @staticmethod
252	    def from_optional(
253	        n: Optional[int] = 1,
254	        best_of: Optional[int] = None,
255	        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
256	        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
257	        repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = 1.0,
258	        temperature: Optional[float] = 1.0,
259	        top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0,
260	        top_k: int = 0,
261	        min_p: float = 0.0,
262	        seed: Optional[int] = None,
263	        stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None,
264	        stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
265	        bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None,
266	        include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False,
267	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
268	        max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16,
269	        min_tokens: int = 0,
270	        logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
271	        prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
272	        detokenize: bool = True,
273	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
274	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
275	        logits_processors: Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] = None,
276	        truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int,
277	                                                   msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None,
278	        output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE,
279	        guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None,
280	        logit_bias: Optional[Union[dict[int, float], dict[str, float]]] = None,
281	        allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
282	        extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
283	    ) -> "SamplingParams":
284	        if logit_bias is not None:
285	            # Convert token_id to integer
286	            # Clamp the bias between -100 and 100 per OpenAI API spec
287	            logit_bias = {
288	                int(token): min(100.0, max(-100.0, bias))
289	                for token, bias in logit_bias.items()
290	            }
291	
292	        return SamplingParams(
293	            n=1 if n is None else n,
294	            best_of=best_of,
295	            presence_penalty=0.0
296	            if presence_penalty is None else presence_penalty,
297	            frequency_penalty=0.0
298	            if frequency_penalty is None else frequency_penalty,
299	            repetition_penalty=1.0
300	            if repetition_penalty is None else repetition_penalty,
301	            temperature=1.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
302	            top_p=1.0 if top_p is None else top_p,
303	            top_k=top_k,
304	            min_p=min_p,
305	            seed=seed,
306	            stop=stop,
307	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
308	            bad_words=bad_words,
309	            include_stop_str_in_output=include_stop_str_in_output,
310	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
311	            max_tokens=max_tokens,
312	            min_tokens=min_tokens,
313	            logprobs=logprobs,
314	            prompt_logprobs=prompt_logprobs,
315	            detokenize=detokenize,
316	            skip_special_tokens=skip_special_tokens,
317	            spaces_between_special_tokens=spaces_between_special_tokens,
318	            logits_processors=logits_processors,
319	            truncate_prompt_tokens=truncate_prompt_tokens,
320	            output_kind=output_kind,
321	            guided_decoding=guided_decoding,
322	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
323	            allowed_token_ids=allowed_token_ids,
324	            extra_args=extra_args,
325	        )
326	
327	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
328	        # how we deal with `best_of``:
329	        # if `best_of`` is not set, we default to `n`;
330	        # if `best_of`` is set, we set `n`` to `best_of`,
331	        # and set `_real_n`` to the original `n`.
332	        # when we return the result, we will check
333	        # if we need to return `n` or `_real_n` results
334	        if self.best_of:
335	            if self.best_of < self.n:
336	                raise ValueError(
337	                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
338	                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
339	            if not self._real_n:
340	                self._real_n = self.n
341	                self.n = self.best_of
342	
343	        if 0 < self.temperature < _MAX_TEMP:
344	            logger.warning(
345	                "temperature %s is less than %s, which may cause numerical "
346	                "errors nan or inf in tensors. We have maxed it out to %s.",
347	                self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP, _MAX_TEMP)
348	            self.temperature = max(self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP)
349	
350	        if self.seed == -1:
351	            self.seed = None
352	
353	        if self.stop is None:
354	            self.stop = []
355	        elif isinstance(self.stop, str):
356	            self.stop = [self.stop]
357	
358	        if self.stop_token_ids is None:
359	            self.stop_token_ids = []
360	
361	        if self.bad_words is None:
362	            self.bad_words = []
363	
364	        if self.logprobs is True:
365	            self.logprobs = 1
366	
367	        if self.prompt_logprobs is True:
368	            self.prompt_logprobs = 1
369	
370	        # Number of characters to hold back for stop string evaluation
371	        # until sequence is finished.
372	        if self.stop and not self.include_stop_str_in_output:
373	            self.output_text_buffer_length = max(len(s) for s in self.stop) - 1
374	
375	        self._verify_args()
376	
377	        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
378	            # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
379	            self.top_p = 1.0
380	            self.top_k = 0
381	            self.min_p = 0.0
382	            self._verify_greedy_sampling()
383	
384	        # eos_token_id is added to this by the engine
385	        self._all_stop_token_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)
386	
387	    def _verify_args(self) -> None:
388	        if not isinstance(self.n, int):
389	            raise ValueError(f"n must be an int, but is of "
390	                             f"type {type(self.n)}")
391	        if self.n < 1:
392	            raise ValueError(f"n must be at least 1, got {self.n}.")
393	        if self.best_of is not None:
394	            if not isinstance(self.best_of, int):
395	                raise ValueError(
396	                    f"best_of must be an integer, got {type(self.best_of)}")
397	            if self.best_of < 1:
398	                raise ValueError(
399	                    f"best_of must be at least 1, got {self.best_of}")
400	            if self.best_of < self.n:
401	                raise ValueError(
402	                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
403	                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
404	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
405	            raise ValueError("presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
406	                             f"{self.presence_penalty}.")
407	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
408	            raise ValueError("frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
409	                             f"{self.frequency_penalty}.")
410	        if self.repetition_penalty <= 0.0:
411	            raise ValueError(
412	                "repetition_penalty must be greater than zero, got "
413	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}.")
414	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
415	            raise ValueError(
416	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}.")
417	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
418	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
419	        # quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
420	        if self.top_k < -1:
421	            raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
422	                             f"got {self.top_k}.")
423	        if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
424	            raise TypeError(
425	                f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
426	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
427	            raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
428	                             f"{self.min_p}.")
429	        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.max_tokens < 1:
430	            raise ValueError(
431	                f"max_tokens must be at least 1, got {self.max_tokens}.")
432	        if self.min_tokens < 0:
433	            raise ValueError(f"min_tokens must be greater than or equal to 0, "
434	                             f"got {self.min_tokens}.")
435	        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.min_tokens > self.max_tokens:
436	            raise ValueError(
437	                f"min_tokens must be less than or equal to "
438	                f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, got {self.min_tokens}.")
439	        if self.logprobs is not None and self.logprobs < 0:
440	            raise ValueError(
441	                f"logprobs must be non-negative, got {self.logprobs}.")
442	        if self.prompt_logprobs is not None and self.prompt_logprobs < 0:
443	            raise ValueError(f"prompt_logprobs must be non-negative, got "
444	                             f"{self.prompt_logprobs}.")
445	        if (self.truncate_prompt_tokens is not None
446	                and self.truncate_prompt_tokens < 1):
447	            raise ValueError(f"truncate_prompt_tokens must be >= 1, "
448	                             f"got {self.truncate_prompt_tokens}")
449	        assert isinstance(self.stop_token_ids, list)
450	        if not all(isinstance(st_id, int) for st_id in self.stop_token_ids):
451	            raise ValueError(f"stop_token_ids must contain only integers, "
452	                             f"got {self.stop_token_ids}.")
453	        assert isinstance(self.stop, list)
454	        if any(not stop_str for stop_str in self.stop):
455	            raise ValueError("stop cannot contain an empty string.")
456	        if self.stop and not self.detokenize:
457	            raise ValueError(
458	                "stop strings are only supported when detokenize is True. "
459	                "Set detokenize=True to use stop.")
460	        if self.best_of != self._real_n and self.output_kind == (
461	                RequestOutputKind.DELTA):
462	            raise ValueError("best_of must equal n to use output_kind=DELTA")
463	
464	    def _verify_greedy_sampling(self) -> None:
465	        if self.n > 1:
466	            raise ValueError("n must be 1 when using greedy sampling, "
467	                             f"got {self.n}.")
468	
469	    def update_from_generation_config(
470	            self,
471	            generation_config: dict[str, Any],
472	            model_eos_token_id: Optional[int] = None) -> None:
473	        """Update if there are non-default values from generation_config"""
474	
475	        if model_eos_token_id is not None:
476	            # Add the eos token id into the sampling_params to support
477	            # min_tokens processing.
478	            self._all_stop_token_ids.add(model_eos_token_id)
479	
480	        # Update eos_token_id for generation
481	        if (eos_ids := generation_config.get("eos_token_id")) is not None:
482	            # it can be either int or list of int
483	            eos_ids = {eos_ids} if isinstance(eos_ids, int) else set(eos_ids)
484	            if model_eos_token_id is not None:
485	                # We don't need to include the primary eos_token_id in
486	                # stop_token_ids since it's handled separately for stopping
487	                # purposes.
488	                eos_ids.discard(model_eos_token_id)
489	            if eos_ids:
490	                self._all_stop_token_ids.update(eos_ids)
491	                if not self.ignore_eos:
492	                    eos_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)
493	                    self.stop_token_ids = list(eos_ids)
494	
495	    def update_from_tokenizer(self, tokenizer: AnyTokenizer) -> None:
496	        if not self.bad_words:
497	            return
498	        self._bad_words_token_ids = []
499	        for bad_word in self.bad_words:
500	            # To prohibit words both at the beginning
501	            # and in the middle of text
502	            # (related to add_prefix_space tokenizer parameter)
503	            for add_prefix_space in [False, True]:
504	                prefix = " " if add_prefix_space else ""
505	                prompt = prefix + bad_word.lstrip()
506	                prompt_token_ids = tokenizer.encode(text=prompt,
507	                                                    add_special_tokens=False)
508	
509	                # If no space at the beginning
510	                # or if prefix space produces a new word token
511	                if (not add_prefix_space) or (
512	                        add_prefix_space and prompt_token_ids[0]
513	                        != self._bad_words_token_ids[-1][0]
514	                        and len(prompt_token_ids) == len(
515	                            self._bad_words_token_ids[-1])):
516	                    self._bad_words_token_ids.append(prompt_token_ids)
517	
518	        invalid_token_ids = [
519	            token_id for bad_words_token_ids in self._bad_words_token_ids
520	            for token_id in bad_words_token_ids
521	            if token_id < 0 or token_id > tokenizer.max_token_id
522	        ]
523	        if len(invalid_token_ids) > 0:
524	            raise ValueError(
525	                f"The model vocabulary size is {tokenizer.max_token_id+1},"
526	                f" but the following tokens"
527	                f" were specified as bad: {invalid_token_ids}."
528	                f" All token id values should be integers satisfying:"
529	                f" 0 <= token_id <= {tokenizer.max_token_id}.")
530	
531	    @cached_property
532	    def sampling_type(self) -> SamplingType:
533	        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
534	            return SamplingType.GREEDY
535	        if self.seed is not None:
536	            return SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED
537	        return SamplingType.RANDOM
538	
539	    @property
540	    def all_stop_token_ids(self) -> set[int]:
541	        return self._all_stop_token_ids
542	
543	    @property
544	    def bad_words_token_ids(self) -> Optional[list[list[int]]]:
545	        # For internal use only. Backward compatibility not guaranteed
546	        return self._bad_words_token_ids
547	
548	    def clone(self) -> "SamplingParams":

558	            id(lp): lp.clone() if hasattr(lp, 'clone') else lp
559	            for lp in self.logits_processors
560	        }
561	        return copy.deepcopy(self, memo=logit_processor_refs)

564	        return (
565	            f"SamplingParams(n={self.n}, "

86	class SamplerOutput(

90	    """For each sequence group, we generate a list of SequenceOutput object,
91	    each of which contains one possible candidate for the next token.
92	
93	    This data structure implements methods, so it can be used like a list, but
94	    also has optional fields for device tensors.
95	    """
96	
97	    outputs: list[CompletionSequenceGroupOutput]

99	    # On-device tensor containing probabilities of each token.

101

107	    # sampler result (muliti-step scheduling)

109	
110	    # On-device tensor containing the sampled token ids.

112	    # CPU tensor containing the sampled token ids. Used during multi-step to
113	    # return the sampled token ids from last rank to AsyncLLMEngine to be

115	    sampled_token_ids_cpu: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
116	
117	    # On-device tensor containing the sampled token embeddings (embeddings
118	    # corresponding to the sampled token ids). Used when prompt embeddings are

120	    sampled_token_embeds: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
121	
122	    # Spec decode metrics populated by workers.

124

131	
132	    # Time taken in the forward pass for this across all workers

134

3	"""Sampler layer implementing TPU supported operations."""
4	
5	import torch
6	import torch.nn as nn
7	
8	from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput
9	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler
10	from vllm.v1.sample.tpu.metadata import TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata
11	
12	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
13	
14	
15	class Sampler(nn.Module):
16	
17	    def __init__(self):
18	        super().__init__()
19	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
20	
21	    def forward(
22	        self,
23	        logits: torch.Tensor,
24	        sampling_metadata: TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata,
25	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
26	        # Use float32 for the logits.
27	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
28	        # Sample the next token.
29	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
30	
31	        # These are TPU tensors.
32	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
33	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
34	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
35	            # token per request.
36	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
37	            logprobs_tensors=None)
38	        return sampler_output
39	
40	    def apply_temperature(
41	        self,
42	        logits: torch.Tensor,
43	        temp: torch.Tensor,
44	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
45	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
46	
47	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
48	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
49	
50	    def sample(
51	        self,
52	        logits: torch.Tensor,
53	        sampling_metadata: TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata,
54	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
55	        greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
56	
57	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
58	
59	        # Apply temperature.
60	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
61	
62	        # Apply min_p.
63	        if sampling_metadata.min_p is not None:
64	            logits = self.apply_min_p(logits, sampling_metadata.min_p)
65	
66	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
67	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
68	            logits,
69	            sampling_metadata.generators,
70	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
71	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
72	        )
73	
74	        sampled = torch.where(sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
75	                              greedy_sampled, random_sampled)
76	        return sampled
77	
78	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
79	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
80	
81	    def gather_logprobs(
82	        self,
83	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
84	        num_logprobs: int,
85	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
86	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
87	        """
88	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
89	
90	        Args:
91	          logits: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
92	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
93	                        retain per token
94	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
95	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
96	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
97	                     with (num tokens) elements
98	
99	        Returns:
100	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
101	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
102	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
103	        """
104	        # Find the topK values.
105	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
106	                                                 num_logprobs,
107	                                                 dim=-1)
108	
109	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
110	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
111	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
112	
113	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
114	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
115	
116	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
117	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
118	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
119	
120	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
121	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
122	
123	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)
124	
125	    def apply_min_p(
126	        self,
127	        logits: torch.Tensor,
128	        min_p: torch.Tensor,
129	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
130	        """
131	        Filters logits using adaptive probability thresholding.
132	        """
133	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
134	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
135	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
136	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
137	                                       dim=-1,
138	                                       keepdim=True)
139	        # Reshape min_p for broadcasting
140	        adjusted_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze(1) * max_probabilities
141	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
142	        valid_token_mask = probability_values >= adjusted_min_p
143	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing (xla friendly)
144	        logits.masked_fill_(~valid_token_mask, -float("inf"))
145	        return logits

332	            sample_logprobs,
333	            on_device_tensors=on_device_tensors,
334	            skip_sampler_cpu_output=sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output)
335	
336	    @property
337	    def _should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace(self) -> bool:
338	        """Whether or not the sampler should modify the probability distribution
339	        of greedily-sampled tokens such that multinomial sampling would sample
340	        the greedily-sampled token.
341	
342	        In other words, if True then we set the probability of the greedily-
343	        sampled token to 1.
344	
345	        This is used by speculative decoding, which requires that the sampling
346	        method be encoded into the probability distribution.
347	        """
348	        return self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace

351	def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
352	    logits: torch.Tensor,
353	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
354	) -> torch.Tensor:
355	    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
356	        have not been generated yet
357	    """
358	    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
359	    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
360	    logits_applied = 0
361	    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
362	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
363	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
364	
365	        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
366	        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
367	            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
368	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
369	            continue
370	
371	        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
372	        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
373	        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
374	        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
375	            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
376	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
377	                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
378	                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
379	                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)
380	
381	            if seqs_to_penalize:
382	                # convert to the index into logits
383	                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
384	                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
385	                logits_to_penalize.extend(
386	                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))
387	
388	    if logits_to_penalize:
389	        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
390	        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
391	        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")
392	
393	    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
394	    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
395	    return logits
396	
397	
398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits
425	
426	
427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits
442

444	def _greedy_sample(
445	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
446	    samples: torch.Tensor,
447	) -> SampleResultType:
448	    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.
449	
450	    Args:
451	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
452	        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
453	            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
454	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
455	    Returns:
456	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
457	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
458	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
459	    """
460	    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
461	    sample_idx = 0
462	    results: SampleResultType = []
463	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
464	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
465	            results.append(([], []))
466	            continue
467	
468	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
469	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
470	        assert num_parent_seqs == 1, (
471	            "Greedy sampling should have only one seq.")
472	        parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
473	        next_token_ids = [samples_lst[sample_idx]]
474	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
475	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
476	    return results

565	def get_pythonized_sample_results(
566	        sample_result_args: SampleResultArgsType) -> SampleResultType:
567	    '''This function consumes GPU-side sampler results and computes
568	    Pythonized CPU-side sampler results (GPU -> CPU sync.)
569	
570	    Single-step scheduling: this function is invoked at sampling-time
571	    for immediate Pythonization.
572	
573	    Multi-step scheduling: Pythonization is deferred until after multiple
574	    GPU-side steps have been completed.
575	
576	    Args:
577	      sample_result_args: GPU-side inputs to the Pythonization process
578	
579	    Returns:
580	      Pythonized sampler results
581	    '''
582	
583	    (
584	        sample_metadata,
585	        sampling_metadata,
586	        greedy_samples,
587	        multinomial_samples,
588	        sample_results_dict,
589	    ) = (
590	        sample_result_args.sample_metadata,
591	        sample_result_args.sampling_metadata,
592	        sample_result_args.greedy_samples,
593	        sample_result_args.multinomial_samples,
594	        sample_result_args.sample_results_dict,
595	    )
596	
597	    for sampling_type in SamplingType:
598	        if sampling_type not in sample_metadata:
599	            continue
600	        (seq_group_id, seq_groups) = sample_metadata[sampling_type]
601	        if sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
602	            sample_results = _greedy_sample(seq_groups, greedy_samples)
603	        elif sampling_type in (SamplingType.RANDOM, SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED):
604	            sample_results = _random_sample(seq_groups,
605	                                            multinomial_samples[sampling_type])
606	        sample_results_dict.update(zip(seq_group_id, sample_results))
607	
608	    return [
609	        sample_results_dict.get(i, ([], []))
610	        for i in range(len(sampling_metadata.seq_groups))
611	    ]

618	    sampling_tensors: SamplingTensors,
619	    include_gpu_probs_tensor: bool,
620	    modify_greedy_probs: bool,

622	    '''Torch-oriented _sample() implementation.
623	
624	    Single-step scheduling:
625	    * Perform GPU-side sampling computation
626	    * Immediately Pythonize sampling result
627	
628	    Multi-step scheduling:
629	    * Perform GPU-side sampling computation
630	    * Defer Pythonization & preserve GPU-side
631	      tensors required for Pythonization
632	    '''
633	
634	    categorized_seq_group_ids: dict[SamplingType, list[int]] = {
635	        t: []
636	        for t in SamplingType
637	    }
638	    categorized_sample_indices = sampling_metadata.categorized_sample_indices
639	    for i, seq_group in enumerate(sampling_metadata.seq_groups):
640	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
641	        sampling_type = sampling_params.sampling_type
642	        categorized_seq_group_ids[sampling_type].append(i)
643	
644	    sample_results_dict: SampleResultsDictType = {}
645	    sample_metadata: SampleMetadataType = {}
646	    multinomial_samples: MultinomialSamplesType = {}
647	    greedy_samples: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
648	
649	    # Create output tensor for sampled token ids.
650	    if include_gpu_probs_tensor:
651	        sampled_token_ids_tensor = torch.full((logprobs.shape[0], 1),
652	                                              VLLM_INVALID_TOKEN_ID,
653	                                              dtype=torch.long,
654	                                              device=logprobs.device)
655	    else:
656	        sampled_token_ids_tensor = None
657	
658	    # Counterintiutively, having two loops here is actually faster.
659	    # The first loop can run without waiting on GPU<->CPU sync.
660	    for sampling_type in SamplingType:
661	        sample_indices = categorized_sample_indices[sampling_type]
662	        num_tokens = len(sample_indices)
663	        if num_tokens == 0:
664	            continue
665	
666	        seq_group_id = categorized_seq_group_ids[sampling_type]
667	        seq_groups = [sampling_metadata.seq_groups[i] for i in seq_group_id]
668	        sample_metadata[sampling_type] = (seq_group_id, seq_groups)
669	        long_sample_indices = sample_indices.long()
670	        if sampling_type == SamplingType.GREEDY:
671	            greedy_samples = torch.argmax(logprobs[long_sample_indices],
672	                                          dim=-1)
673	
674	            if sampled_token_ids_tensor is not None:
675	                # Store sampled tokens in output tensor.
676	                sampled_token_ids_tensor[
677	                    long_sample_indices] = greedy_samples.unsqueeze(-1)
678	
679	            if modify_greedy_probs:
680	                # If required, modify the probabilities such that sampling from
681	                # the modified distribution would always sample the argmax
682	                # token id.
683	                _modify_greedy_probs_inplace(logprobs, probs,
684	                                             long_sample_indices,
685	                                             greedy_samples)
686	
687	        elif sampling_type in (SamplingType.RANDOM, SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED):
688	            max_n_in_batch = 1
689	            for seq_group in seq_groups:
690	                if seq_group.is_prompt:
691	                    sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
692	                    max_n_in_batch = max(max_n_in_batch, sampling_params.n)
693	            seq_groups_arg = (None if sampling_type == SamplingType.RANDOM else
694	                              seq_groups)
695	
696	            if flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is not None:
697	                logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
698	                               "per-request generators. Falling back to "

20	class SequenceGroupToSample:
21	    # |---------- N-1 iteration --------|
22	    # |---------------- N iteration ---------------------|
23	    # |- tokenA -|......................|-- newTokens ---|
24	    # |---------- context_len ----------|
25	    # |-------------------- seq_len ----------------------|
26	    #                                   |-- query_len ---|
27	
28	    # Sequence ids for the sequence group in a previous step.
29	    seq_ids: list[int]
30	    sampling_params: SamplingParams
31	    # seq_id -> sequence data.
32	    seq_data: dict[int, SequenceData]
33	    # The length of the sequence (all tokens seen in the past + new token to
34	    # compute attention) of the sequence group. None if it is in a decode
35	    # stage.
36	    seq_len: Optional[int]
37	    # The length of new query tokens to compute in the current step. None if it
38	    # is in a decode stage. The length of query_len <= seq_len if chunked
39	    # prefill is enabled.
40	    query_len: Optional[int]
41	    # A random number generator for sampling.
42	    generator: Optional[torch.Generator]
43	    # True if the sequence group is in prefill stage. False if it is in a
44	    # decode stage.
45	    is_prompt: bool
46	    # Query token indices from logits. to compute prompt logprob. Empty if
47	    # prompt logprob is not required.
48	    prompt_logprob_indices: list[int]
49	    # Sample token indices from logits. Empty if sampling is not required.
50	    sample_indices: list[int]

79	    """Used to cache SamplingMetadata objects between scheduler iterations"""

85	        if num_seqs not in self._seq_group_to_sample_cache:
86	            self._seq_group_to_sample_cache[num_seqs] = PyObjectCache(

93	        for cache in self._seq_group_to_sample_cache.values():

372	@dataclass
373	class SamplingTensors:
374	    """Tensors for sampling."""
375	
376	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
377	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
378	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
379	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
380	    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
381	    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
382	    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
383	    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
384	    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
385	
386	    @classmethod
387	    def from_sampling_metadata(
388	        cls,
389	        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
390	        vocab_size: int,
391	        device: torch.device,
392	        dtype: torch.dtype,
393	    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
394	        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
395	        output_tokens: list[array] = []
396	        top_ks: list[int] = []
397	        temperatures: list[float] = []
398	        top_ps: list[float] = []
399	        min_ps: list[float] = []
400	        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
401	        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
402	        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
403	        do_penalties = False
404	        do_top_p_top_k = False
405	        do_min_p = False
406	
407	        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
408	        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
409	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
410	            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
411	            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
412	            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
413	            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
414	            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
415	            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
416	            min_p = sampling_params.min_p
417	
418	            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
419	            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
420	            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
421	            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
422	                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
423	                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
424	                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
425	                temperature = 1.0
426	            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
427	                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
428	                do_top_p_top_k = True
429	            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
430	                do_min_p = True
431	            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
432	                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
433	                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
434	                do_penalties = True
435	
436	            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
437	            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
438	                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
439	                # their logprobs
440	                query_len = seq_group.query_len
441	                assert query_len is not None
442	                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
443	                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
444	                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
445	                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
446	                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
447	                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
448	                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
449	                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len
450	
451	            if seq_group.do_sample:
452	                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
453	                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
454	                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens

## vLLM Sampling Parameters Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored the vLLM codebase and found the sampling parameter definitions. Here's a comprehensive report:

### 1. **File Locations**

- **Main sampling parameters:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775260877668-fktqcc/vllm/sampling_params.py` (603 lines)
- **Sampling metadata (v0):** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775260877668-fktqcc/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (598 lines)
- **Sampling metadata (v1):** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775260877668-fktqcc/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` (44 lines)

---

### 2. **SamplingParams Class Definition**

**File:** `vllm/sampling_params.py`

**Class Structure:**
- Uses `msgspec.Struct` with `omit_defaults=True` and `dict=True`
- Contains 27+ fields with validation in `__post_init__()` method
- Includes both sampling configuration and internal state fields

**top_p Parameter:**
- **Field definition (line 212):** `top_p: float = 1.0`
- **Function parameter (line 259):** `top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0`
- **Docstring (lines 150-151):**
  ```
  top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
      to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
  ```
- **Validation code (lines 417-418):**
  ```python
  if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
      raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
  ```
- **Validation constraint:** Strictly greater than 0 and less than or equal to 1.0

**top_k Parameter:**
- **Field definition (line 213):** `top_k: int = 0`
- **Function parameter (line 260):** `top_k: int = 0`
- **Docstring (lines 152-153):**
  ```
  top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
      to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
  ```
- **Validation code (lines 420-425):**
  ```python
  # quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
  if self.top_k < -1:
      raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
                       f"got {self.top_k}.")
  if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
      raise TypeError(
          f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
  ```
- **Validation constraints:** Must be integer; must be -1, 0, or >= 1; cannot be < -1

**Special Behavior - Greedy Sampling Reset:**
When temperature < 1e-5 (greedy sampling mode), in `__post_init__()` (lines 377-382):
```python
if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
    # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
    self.top_p = 1.0
    self.top_k = 0
    self.min_p = 0.0
    self._verify_greedy_sampling()
```
Both parameters are reset to their defaults for greedy sampling.

---

### 3. **SamplingMetadata Class (v0 - model_executor)**

**File:** `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (lines 97-196)

**Purpose:** Metadata for input sequences used in the sampler. Contains indices and categorization for sampling operations.

**Class Fields:**
```python
class SamplingMetadata:
    seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample]
    selected_token_indices: torch.Tensor
    categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, torch.Tensor]
    num_prompts: int
    skip_sampler_cpu_output: bool = False
    reuse_sampling_tensors: bool = False
```

**Key Methods:**
- `__init__()`: Initialize with sequence groups and indices
- `prepare()`: Static method to prepare metadata from sequence group metadata lists
- `__repr__()`: String representation

---

### 4. **SamplingTensors Class (v0 - model_executor)**

**File:** `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (lines 372-598)

**Purpose:** PyTorch tensors containing batched sampling parameters for all sequences.

**Class Fields** (all torch.Tensor):
```python
@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor
    top_ks: torch.Tensor
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
```

**Key Methods:**
- `from_sampling_metadata()`: Class method to construct from SamplingMetadata (lines 387-495)
  - Returns tuple: `(SamplingTensors, bool, bool, bool)` - the bools indicate if penalties, top_p/top_k, and min_p should be applied
  - Iterates through sequence groups and batches parameters
  - Handles special cases for prompt logprobs and temperature edge cases
  
- `from_lists()`: Class method to create tensors from Python lists (lines 498-597)
  - Creates pinned memory tensors for CPU-GPU transfer
  - Handles empty tensor cases
  - Does non-blocking transfer to device

**top_k/top_p Handling in `from_sampling_metadata()` (lines 418-428):**
```python
# k should not be greater than the vocab size.
top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
    # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
    # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
    # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
    temperature = 1.0
if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                           or top_k != vocab_size):
    do_top_p_top_k = True
```

---

### 5. **SamplingMetadata Class (v1 - v1/sample)**

**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` (lines 12-44)

**Purpose:** Simplified metadata structure for v1 sampling implementation.

**Class Fields:**
```python
@dataclass
class SamplingMetadata:
    temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    all_greedy: bool
    all_random: bool
    
    top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator]
    
    # None means no logprobs, 0 means sampled token logprobs only
    max_num_logprobs: Optional[int]
    
    no_penalties: bool
    prompt_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    
    output_token_ids: list[list[int]]
    
    # `allowed_token_ids_mask` is a 2D bool tensor of shape (max batch size,
    # vocab size).
    allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    
    # req_index -> bad_words_token_ids
    bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]]
    
    # Loaded logits processors
    logitsprocs: LogitsProcessorManager
```

---

### 6. **SequenceGroupToSample Class**

**File:** `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (lines 19-62)

**Purpose:** Represents a sequence group ready for sampling within a batch.

**Class Fields:**
```python
@dataclass
class SequenceGroupToSample:
    seq_ids: list[int]
    sampling_params: SamplingParams
    seq_data: dict[int, SequenceData]
    seq_len: Optional[int]
    query_len: Optional[int]
    generator: Optional[torch.Generator]
    is_prompt: bool
    prompt_logprob_indices: list[int]
    sample_indices: list[int]
```

---

### 7. **vLLM Top-Level Directory Structure**

```
/vllm/
├── adapter_commons/
├── assets/
├── attention/
├── benchmarks/
├── compilation/
├── config.py
├── core/
├── device_allocator/
├── distributed/
├── engine/
├── entrypoints/
├── executor/
├── inputs/
├── logging_utils/
├── lora/
├── model_executor/
├── multimodal/
├── platforms/
├── plugins/
├── pooling_params.py
├── profiler/
├── prompt_adapter/
├── reasoning/
├── sampling_params.py
├── scalar_type.py
├── sequence.py
├── spec_decode/
├── test_utils.py
├── third_party/
├── transformers_utils/
├── triton_utils/
├── usage/
├── utils/
├── v1/
└── worker/
```

---

### 8. **Key Constants**

From `sampling_params.py`:
```python
_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5      # Epsilon for floating-point comparisons
_MAX_TEMP = 1e-2          # Maximum temperature clipping for numerical stability
```

---

### 9. **Summary Table: top_p and top_k Parameters**

| Aspect | top_p | top_k |
|--------|-------|-------|
| **Type** | float | int |
| **Default** | 1.0 | 0 |
| **Valid Range** | (0, 1] | -1, 0, or >= 1 |
| **Disabled Value** | 1.0 | 0 or -1 |
| **Purpose** | Cumulative probability cutoff | Number of tokens to consider |
| **Validation** | `0.0 < top_p <= 1.0` | `top_k >= -1 and isinstance(top_k, int)` |
| **In SamplingTensors** | top_ps (batched) | top_ks (batched) |
| **In v1 SamplingMetadata** | Optional[torch.Tensor] | Optional[torch.Tensor] |

This completes the comprehensive exploration of vLLM's sampling parameter definitions and validation logic.

  - Does non-blocking transfer to device

**top_k/top_p Handling in `from_sampling_metadata()` (lines 418-428):**
```python
# k should not be greater than the vocab size.
top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
    # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
    # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
    # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
    temperature = 1.0
if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                           or top_k != vocab_size):
    do_top_p_top_k = True
```

### 5. **SamplingMetadata Class (v1 - v1/sample)**

**File:** `vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` (lines 12-44)

**Purpose:** Simplified metadata structure for v1 sampling implementation.

**Class Fields:**

Perfect! Now let me generate a comprehensive summary report based on all the information I've gathered. Let me compile my findings:

## COMPREHENSIVE ANALYSIS OF TOP_K AND TOP_P SAMPLING IMPLEMENTATION IN vLLM

Based on my exploration of the vLLM codebase, here's the complete analysis of how top_k and top_p sampling filters are implemented:

### 1. DATA FLOW FROM SAMPLINGPARAMS TO FILTERING CODE

**Entry Point: `SamplingParams` (vllm/sampling_params.py)**
- `top_p: float = 1.0` (default, must be in (0, 1])
- `top_k: int = 0` (default, 0 or -1 disables; can be 1+)
- These are validated in `_verify_args()` method (lines 417-428)
- When temperature < epsilon (greedy sampling), both are reset to defaults (lines 377-381)

### 2. V0 SAMPLER IMPLEMENTATION (vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py)

#### A. The Main Sampling Flow:

The `Sampler` class (line 169-349) has the main forward pass that:

1. **Initialize sampling tensors** (lines 199-223): `_init_sampling_tensors()` calls `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()`
   - Returns: `sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p`
   - Key: Creates tensors on GPU with pinned memory

2. **Apply penalties** (lines 272-277)

3. **Temperature scaling** (lines 281-282)

4. **Apply top_k and top_p filtering** (lines 284-286):
   ```python
   if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
       logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                   sampling_tensors.top_ks)
   ```

5. **Compute probabilities** (line 293)

6. **Sample tokens** (lines 298-305)

#### B. The Core Filtering Functions:

**`_apply_top_k_top_p()` function (lines 398-424):**
```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    # Sort logits in ascending order
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    # TOP-K FILTERING:
    # Calculate the threshold index (vocab_size - k)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    # Get the cutoff logit value at position k
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    # Mask all logits below the k-th value
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # TOP-P FILTERING:
    # Convert to probabilities
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    # Compute cumulative probabilities
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    # Mask tokens where cumsum <= 1 - p
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    # Ensure at least one token remains
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Re-sort probabilities back to original order
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**Key insight:** This is applied BEFORE softmax. The logits are sorted, filtered by setting unwanted tokens to -inf, then rescattered to original positions.

#### C. SamplingTensors Creation:

**`SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` (lines 386-495 in sampling_metadata.py):**
1. Extracts `top_p` and `top_k` from each sequence group's `SamplingParams`
2. Clamps `top_k` to vocab_size: `top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)`
3. If `top_k < 1`, sets it to `vocab_size` (disables top_k)
4. Creates lists of values: `top_ks: list[int]` and `top_ps: list[float]`
5. **Key logic** (lines 426-428):
   ```python
   if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                              or top_k != vocab_size):
       do_top_p_top_k = True
   ```
   - Sets flag if ANY sequence needs filtering

6. **`from_lists()` creates GPU tensors** (lines 498-597):
   - `top_ps_t = torch.tensor(top_ps, device="cpu", dtype=dtype, pin_memory=pin_memory)`
   - `top_ks_t = torch.tensor(top_ks, device="cpu", dtype=torch.int, pin_memory=pin_memory)`
   - Transfers to GPU: `.to(device=device, non_blocking=True)`

### 3. V1 SAMPLER IMPLEMENTATION (vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py)

The V1 sampler has more optimized paths with three implementations:

#### A. PyTorch Native Implementation (`forward_native`, lines 69-83):
```python
def forward_native(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
    return random_sample(probs, generators)
```

#### B. CUDA with FlashInfer (`forward_cuda`, lines 85-107):
```python
def forward_cuda(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if k is None and p is None:
        # No filtering needed - faster path
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        return random_sample(probs, generators)
    if generators:
        # Fall back to native if per-request generators
        return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
    # Use FlashInfer for GPU-optimized sampling
    return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
```

#### C. TPU Optimized (`forward_tpu`, lines 109-118):
```python
def forward_tpu(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
    return random_sample(probs, generators)
```

#### D. The Core V1 Filtering Functions:

**`apply_top_k_top_p()` (lines 168-208):**
Similar to V0 but with cleaner structure:
```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if p is None:
        if k is None:
            return logits
        # Top-k only - avoid sorting entire vocab
        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
    
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    if k is not None:
        # Apply top-k
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    if p is not None:
        # Apply top-p
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Re-sort
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**`apply_top_k_only()` (lines 211-233):**
Optimized path that avoids full vocab sort:
```python
def apply_top_k_only(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
    max_top_k = k.max()
    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**`apply_top_k_top_p_tpu()` (lines 121-165):**
TPU-optimized version that avoids scatter operations:
```python
def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    if k is not None:
        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
    
    if p is not None:
        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
    
    return logits
```

#### E. FlashInfer GPU Sampling (`flashinfer_sample()`, lines 260-296):
```python
def flashinfer_sample(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    assert not (k is None and p is None)
    if k is None:
        # Top-p only
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, p, deterministic=True)
    elif p is None:
        # Top-k only
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, k, deterministic=True)
    else:
        # Both top-k and top-p
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
```

### 4. ALL FILE PATHS CONTAINING TOP_K/TOP_P REFERENCES

Based on grep results, these are the key files:

**Core Sampling:**
- `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` - V0 main sampler (1204 lines)
- `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` - V1 main sampler
- `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` - V1 filtering ops
- `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` - Metadata and tensor creation
- `/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` - V1 metadata
- `/vllm/sampling_params.py` - SamplingParams definition

**TPU-Specific:**
- `/vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py` - TPU sampler
- `/vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py` - TPU metadata

**Workers/Models (89 total files with references):**
- `/vllm/worker/model_runner.py`
- `/vllm/worker/tpu_model_runner.py`
- `/vllm/worker/neuron_model_runner.py`
- `/vllm/worker/gpu_model_runner.py`
- And many model-specific files

**Other Components:**
- `/vllm/spec_decode/spec_decode_worker.py`
- `/vllm/model_executor/models/*` (MOE models)
- `/vllm/engine/llm_engine.py`

### 5. KEY EXECUTION FLOW SUMMARY

```
SamplingParams (top_k, top_p)
    ↓
SequenceGroupToSample (carries sampling_params)
    ↓
SamplingMetadata.from_seq_groups()
    ↓
SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()
    - Extracts top_k, top_p from each seq_group
    - Creates GPU tensors with shape [batch_size]
    - Sets do_top_p_top_k flag if filtering needed
    ↓
Sampler.forward()
    - Temperature scaling on logits
    - IF do_top_p_top_k:
        - Call _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, top_ps, top_ks)
        - OR Use FlashInfer GPU kernels
    - Compute probabilities via softmax
    - Multinomial sampling from filtered probs
    ↓
Token ID output
```

### 6. KEY IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

1. **Filtering happens BEFORE softmax** - Logits are masked to -inf, then converted to probabilities
2. **Top-k algorithm**: Sort logits, find k-th largest value, mask everything below it
3. **Top-p algorithm**: Convert to probs, compute cumsum, mask where cumsum <= 1-p
4. **Optimization**: Top-k only path avoids full vocab sort using topk() directly
5. **GPU acceleration**: FlashInfer provides highly optimized CUDA kernels (lines 284-294 V0, lines 260-296 V1)
6. **Tensor batching**: Each batch element can have different top_k/top_p values
7. **Min-p support**: Additional filtering via `_apply_min_p()` (lines 427-441 V0, line 427-441)
8. **Special handling for greedy**: When temp < epsilon, top_k/top_p are disabled by SamplingParams.__post_init__

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me generate a comprehensive summary report based on all the information I've gathered. Let me compile my findings:

## COMPREHENSIVE ANALYSIS OF TOP_K AND TOP_P SAMPLING IMPLEMENTATION IN vLLM

Based on my exploration of the vLLM codebase, here's the complete analysis of how top_k and top_p sampling filters are implemented:

### 1. DATA FLOW FROM SAMPLINGPARAMS TO FILTERING CODE

**Entry Point: `SamplingParams` (vllm/sampling_params.py)**
- `top_p: float = 1.0` (default, must be in (0, 1])
- `top_k: int = 0` (default, 0 or -1 disables; can be 1+)
- These are validated in `_verify_args()` method (lines 417-428)
- When temperature < epsilon (greedy sampling), both are reset to defaults (lines 377-381)

### 2. V0 SAMPLER IMPLEMENTATION (vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py)

#### A. The Main Sampling Flow:

The `Sampler` class (line 169-349) has the main forward pass that:

1. **Initialize sampling tensors** (lines 199-223): `_init_sampling_tensors()` calls `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()`
   - Returns: `sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p`
   - Key: Creates tensors on GPU with pinned memory

2. **Apply penalties** (lines 272-277)

3. **Temperature scaling** (lines 281-282)

4. **Apply top_k and top_p filtering** (lines 284-286):
   ```python
   if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
       logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                   sampling_tensors.top_ks)
   ```

5. **Compute probabilities** (line 293)

6. **Sample tokens** (lines 298-305)

#### B. The Core Filtering Functions:

**`_apply_top_k_top_p()` function (lines 398-424):**
```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    # Sort logits in ascending order
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    # TOP-K FILTERING:
    # Calculate the threshold index (vocab_size - k)
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    # Get the cutoff logit value at position k
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    # Mask all logits below the k-th value
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # TOP-P FILTERING:
    # Convert to probabilities
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    # Compute cumulative probabilities
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    # Mask tokens where cumsum <= 1 - p
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    # Ensure at least one token remains
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Re-sort probabilities back to original order
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**Key insight:** This is applied BEFORE softmax. The logits are sorted, filtered by setting unwanted tokens to -inf, then rescattered to original positions.

#### C. SamplingTensors Creation:

**`SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` (lines 386-495 in sampling_metadata.py):**
1. Extracts `top_p` and `top_k` from each sequence group's `SamplingParams`
2. Clamps `top_k` to vocab_size: `top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)`
3. If `top_k < 1`, sets it to `vocab_size` (disables top_k)
4. Creates lists of values: `top_ks: list[int]` and `top_ps: list[float]`
5. **Key logic** (lines 426-428):
   ```python
   if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                              or top_k != vocab_size):
       do_top_p_top_k = True
   ```
   - Sets flag if ANY sequence needs filtering

6. **`from_lists()` creates GPU tensors** (lines 498-597):
   - `top_ps_t = torch.tensor(top_ps, device="cpu", dtype=dtype, pin_memory=pin_memory)`
   - `top_ks_t = torch.tensor(top_ks, device="cpu", dtype=torch.int, pin_memory=pin_memory)`
   - Transfers to GPU: `.to(device=device, non_blocking=True)`

### 3. V1 SAMPLER IMPLEMENTATION (vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py)

The V1 sampler has more optimized paths with three implementations:

#### A. PyTorch Native Implementation (`forward_native`, lines 69-83):
```python
def forward_native(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
    return random_sample(probs, generators)
```

#### B. CUDA with FlashInfer (`forward_cuda`, lines 85-107):
```python
def forward_cuda(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if k is None and p is None:
        # No filtering needed - faster path
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        return random_sample(probs, generators)
    if generators:
        # Fall back to native if per-request generators
        return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
    # Use FlashInfer for GPU-optimized sampling
    return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
```

#### C. TPU Optimized (`forward_tpu`, lines 109-118):
```python
def forward_tpu(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
    return random_sample(probs, generators)
```

#### D. The Core V1 Filtering Functions:

**`apply_top_k_top_p()` (lines 168-208):**
Similar to V0 but with cleaner structure:
```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if p is None:
        if k is None:
            return logits
        # Top-k only - avoid sorting entire vocab
        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
    
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    if k is not None:
        # Apply top-k
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    if p is not None:
        # Apply top-p
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
    
    # Re-sort
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

**`apply_top_k_only()` (lines 211-233):**
Optimized path that avoids full vocab sort:
```python
def apply_top_k_only(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
    max_top_k = k.max()
    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

**`apply_top_k_top_p_tpu()` (lines 121-165):**
TPU-optimized version that avoids scatter operations:
```python
def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
    
    if k is not None:
        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
    
    if p is not None:
        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
    
    return logits
```

#### E. FlashInfer GPU Sampling (`flashinfer_sample()`, lines 260-296):
```python
def flashinfer_sample(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    assert not (k is None and p is None)
    if k is None:
        # Top-p only
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, p, deterministic=True)
    elif p is None:
        # Top-k only
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, k, deterministic=True)
    else:
        # Both top-k and top-p
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
```

### 4. ALL FILE PATHS CONTAINING TOP_K/TOP_P REFERENCES

Based on grep results, these are the key files:

**Core Sampling:**
- `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` - V0 main sampler (1204 lines)
- `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` - V1 main sampler
- `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` - V1 filtering ops
- `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` - Metadata and tensor creation
- `/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` - V1 metadata
- `/vllm/sampling_params.py` - SamplingParams definition

**TPU-Specific:**
- `/vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py` - TPU sampler
- `/vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py` - TPU metadata

**Workers/Models (89 total files with references):**
- `/vllm/worker/model_runner.py`
- `/vllm/worker/tpu_model_runner.py`
- `/vllm/worker/neuron_model_runner.py`
- `/vllm/worker/gpu_model_runner.py`
- And many model-specific files

**Other Components:**
- `/vllm/spec_decode/spec_decode_worker.py`
- `/vllm/model_executor/models/*` (MOE models)
- `/vllm/engine/llm_engine.py`

### 5. KEY EXECUTION FLOW SUMMARY

```
SamplingParams (top_k, top_p)
    ↓
SequenceGroupToSample (carries sampling_params)
    ↓
SamplingMetadata.from_seq_groups()
    ↓
SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()
    - Extracts top_k, top_p from each seq_group
    - Creates GPU tensors with shape [batch_size]
    - Sets do_top_p_top_k flag if filtering needed
    ↓
Sampler.forward()
    - Temperature scaling on logits
    - IF do_top_p_top_k:
        - Call _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, top_ps, top_ks)
        - OR Use FlashInfer GPU kernels
    - Compute probabilities via softmax
    - Multinomial sampling from filtered probs
    ↓
Token ID output
```

### 6. KEY IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

1. **Filtering happens BEFORE softmax** - Logits are masked to -inf, then converted to probabilities
2. **Top-k algorithm**: Sort logits, find k-th largest value, mask everything below it
3. **Top-p algorithm**: Convert to probs, compute cumsum, mask where cumsum <= 1-p
4. **Optimization**: Top-k only path avoids full vocab sort using topk() directly
5. **GPU acceleration**: FlashInfer provides highly optimized CUDA kernels (lines 284-294 V0, lines 260-296 V1)
6. **Tensor batching**: Each batch element can have different top_k/top_p values
7. **Min-p support**: Additional filtering via `_apply_min_p()` (lines 427-441 V0, line 427-441)
8. **Special handling for greedy**: When temp < epsilon, top_k/top_p are disabled by SamplingParams.__post_init__
