117	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
118	    logits: torch.Tensor,
119	    k: torch.Tensor,
120	    p: torch.Tensor,
121	) -> torch.Tensor:
122	    """
123	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
123	
124	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
125	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
126	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
127	    shall constitute the top-p set.
128	
129	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
130	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
131	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
132	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
133	    being broken then.
134	    """
135	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
136	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
137	
138	    if k is not None:
139	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
139	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
140	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
141	
142	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
143	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
144	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
144	
145	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
146	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
147	
148	    if p is not None:
149	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
150	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
151	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
152	
153	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
154	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
155	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
156	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
157	
158	    return logits
159	
160	
161	def apply_top_k_top_p(
162	    logits: torch.Tensor,
163	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
163	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
164	) -> torch.Tensor:
165	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
166	
166	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
167	    which can be slow for large batches.
168	
169	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
170	    """
171	    if p is None:
172	        if k is None:
172	            return logits
173	
174	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
175	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
176	
177	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
178	
179	    if k is not None:
179	        # Apply top-k.
180	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
181	        # Get all the top_k values.
182	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
183	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
184	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
185	
186	    if p is not None:
186	        # Apply top-p.
187	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
188	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
189	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
190	        # at least one
191	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
192	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
193	

194	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
195	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
196	    return logits
197	
198	
199	def apply_top_k_only(
200	    logits: torch.Tensor,
201	    k: torch.Tensor,
202	) -> torch.Tensor:
203	    """
204	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
204	
205	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
206	
207	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
208	    """
209	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
210	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
211	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
212	    max_top_k = k.max()
213	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
213	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
214	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
215	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
216	    # Handle non-topk rows.
216	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
217	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
218	    return logits
219	

220	def random_sample(
221	    probs: torch.Tensor,
221	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
222	) -> torch.Tensor:
223	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
223	
224	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
225	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
226	    """
227	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
228	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
228	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
229	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
230	    # that have their own seeds.
231	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
231	        q.exponential_()
232	    if generators:
232	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
233	        # one by one. Optimize this.
234	        for i, generator in generators.items():
235	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
236	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
237	

238	
239	def flashinfer_sample(
240	    logits: torch.Tensor,
240	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
241	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
242	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
242	) -> torch.Tensor:
243	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
243	
244	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
245	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
246	    via rejection sampling.
247	
248	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
248	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
249	    statistically equivalent.
250	
251	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
251	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
252	    the synchronization overhead.
252	    """
253	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
253	    if k is None:
254	        # Top-p only.
255	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
256	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
256	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
257	    elif p is None:
257	        # Top-k only.
258	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
259	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
259	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
260	    else:
260	        # Both top-k and top-p.
261	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
261	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
262	
263	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
264

42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
43	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
44	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
45	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
46	                    # why we use the condition
47	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
48	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
49	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
50	                else:
51	                    logger.warning(
52	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
53	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
54	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
55	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
56	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
57	            else:
58	                logger.warning(
59	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
60	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "

62	                self.forward = self.forward_native

68	    def forward_native(
69	        self,
70	        logits: torch.Tensor,
71	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
72	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
73	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
75	        """
76	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
77	
77	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
78	        """
79	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
80	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
81	        return random_sample(probs, generators)

83	    def forward_cuda(
84	        self,
85	        logits: torch.Tensor,
86	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
87	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
88	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
89	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
90	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
91	        if k is None and p is None:
92	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
92	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
93	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
94	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
95	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
96	        if generators:
97	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
98	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "

102	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
103	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
104	
105	    def forward_tpu(
106	        self,
107	        logits: torch.Tensor,
108	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
109	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
110	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
111	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
112	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
113	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)

115	
116	
117	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
118	    logits: torch.Tensor,
119	    k: torch.Tensor,
120	    p: torch.Tensor,
121	) -> torch.Tensor:
122	    """
123	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
123	
124	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
125	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
126	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
127	    shall constitute the top-p set.
128	
129	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
130	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
131	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
132	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
133	    being broken then.
134	    """
135	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
136	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
137	
138	    if k is not None:
139	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
139	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
140	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
141	
142	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
143	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
144	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
144	
145	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
146	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
147	
148	    if p is not None:
149	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
150	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
151	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
152	
153	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
154	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
155	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
156	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
157	
158	    return logits
159	
160	
161	def apply_top_k_top_p(
162	    logits: torch.Tensor,
163	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
163	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
164	) -> torch.Tensor:
165	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
166	
166	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
167	    which can be slow for large batches.
168	
169	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
170	    """
171	    if p is None:
172	        if k is None:
172	            return logits
173	
174	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
175	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
176	
177	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
178	
179	    if k is not None:
179	        # Apply top-k.
180	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
181	        # Get all the top_k values.
182	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
183	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
184	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
185	
186	    if p is not None:
186	        # Apply top-p.
187	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
188	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
189	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
190	        # at least one
191	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
192	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
193	

194	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
195	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
196	    return logits
197	
198	
199	def apply_top_k_only(
200	    logits: torch.Tensor,
201	    k: torch.Tensor,
202	) -> torch.Tensor:
203	    """
204	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
204	
205	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
206	
207	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
208	    """
209	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
210	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
211	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
212	    max_top_k = k.max()
213	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
213	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
214	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
215	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
216	    # Handle non-topk rows.
216	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
217	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
218	    return logits
219	

220	def random_sample(
221	    probs: torch.Tensor,
221	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
222	) -> torch.Tensor:
223	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
223	
224	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
225	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
226	    """
227	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
228	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
228	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
229	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
230	    # that have their own seeds.
231	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
231	        q.exponential_()
232	    if generators:
232	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
233	        # one by one. Optimize this.
234	        for i, generator in generators.items():
235	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
236	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
237	

238	
239	def flashinfer_sample(
240	    logits: torch.Tensor,
240	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
241	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
242	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
242	) -> torch.Tensor:
243	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
243	
244	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
245	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
246	    via rejection sampling.
247	
248	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
248	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
249	    statistically equivalent.
250	
251	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
251	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
252	    the synchronization overhead.
252	    """
253	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
253	    if k is None:
254	        # Top-p only.
255	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
256	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
256	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
257	    elif p is None:
257	        # Top-k only.
258	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
259	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
259	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
260	    else:
260	        # Both top-k and top-p.
261	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
261	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
262	
263	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
264

97	        The various logits processing functions called in this method
98	        may update the logits tensor in-place.
99	        """
100	
101	        assert not (sampling_metadata.all_greedy
102	                    and sampling_metadata.all_random)
103	        if sampling_metadata.all_random:
104	            greedy_sampled = None
105	        else:
106	            greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
107	            if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
108	                return greedy_sampled
109	
110	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
111	
112	        # Apply temperature.
113	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
114	
115	        # Apply logits processors that only apply to random sampling
116	        # (argmax invariant)
117	        for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
118	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
119	
120	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
121	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
122	            logits,
123	            sampling_metadata.generators,
124	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
125	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
126	        )
127	
128	        if greedy_sampled is None:
129	            return random_sampled
130	
131	        sampled = torch.where(
132	            sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
133	            greedy_sampled,
134	            random_sampled,
135	            out=greedy_sampled,  # Reuse tensor
136	        )
137	        return sampled
138	
139	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
140	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
141	
142	    def gather_logprobs(
143	        self,
144	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
145	        num_logprobs: int,
146	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
147	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
148	        """
149	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
150	
151	        Args:
152	          logprobs: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
153	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
154	                        retain per token
155	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
156	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
157	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
158	                     with (num tokens) elements
159	                     Must be int64.
160	
161	        Returns:
162	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
163	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
164	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
165	        """
166	        assert token_ids.dtype == torch.int64
167	        # Find the topK values.
168	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
169	                                                 num_logprobs,
170	                                                 dim=-1)
171	
172	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
173	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
174	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
175	
176	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
177	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
178	
179	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
180	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
181	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
182	
183	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
184	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
185	
186	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)
187	
188	    def apply_penalties(
189	        self,
190	        logits: torch.Tensor,
191	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
192	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
193	        if not sampling_metadata.no_penalties:
194	            assert sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids is not None
195	            logits = apply_all_penalties(
196	                logits,
197	                sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids,
198	                sampling_metadata.presence_penalties,
199	                sampling_metadata.frequency_penalties,
200	                sampling_metadata.repetition_penalties,
201	                sampling_metadata.output_token_ids,
202	            )
203	        return logits
204	
205	    def apply_allowed_token_ids(
206	        self,
207	        logits: torch.Tensor,
208	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
209	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
210	        if sampling_metadata.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None:
211	            logits.masked_fill_(sampling_metadata.allowed_token_ids_mask,
212	                                float("-inf"))
213	        return logits
214	
215	    def apply_bad_words(
216	        self,
217	        logits: torch.Tensor,
218	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
219	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
220	        if sampling_metadata.bad_words_token_ids:
221	            apply_bad_words(
222	                logits,
223	                sampling_metadata.bad_words_token_ids,
224	                sampling_metadata.output_token_ids,
225	            )
226	        return logits

388	        """This method must always be followed by a call to condense().

489	    def condense(self) -> None:
490	        """Slide non-empty requests down into lower, empty indices.
491	
492	        Any consecutive empty indices at the very end of the list are not
493	        filled.
494	
495	        Args:
496	          empty_req_indices: empty indices which may be filled.
497	
498	        Returns:
499	          swaps: list of (from,to) swap tuples for moved requests
500	          empty_req_indices: indices not filled by condensation
501	        """
502	        if not (empty_req_indices := self.batch_update_builder.removed):
503	            # All removed requests were replaced by added requests, or else no
504	            # requests were removed at all. No condense() needed
505	            return
506	        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
507	        if num_reqs == 0:
508	            # The batched states are empty.
509	            self._req_ids.clear()
510	            self.req_output_token_ids.clear()
511	            return
512	
513	        # NOTE(woosuk): This function assumes that the empty_req_indices
514	        # is sorted in descending order.
515	        last_req_index = num_reqs + len(empty_req_indices) - 1
516	        while empty_req_indices:
517	            # Find the largest non-empty index.
518	            while last_req_index in empty_req_indices:
519	                last_req_index -= 1
520	
521	            # Find the smallest empty index.
522	            empty_index = self.batch_update_builder.peek_removed()
523	            assert empty_index is not None
524	            if empty_index >= last_req_index:
525	                break
526	
527	            # Move active request down into empty request
528	            # index.
529	            self.batch_update_builder.pop_removed()
530	            self.batch_update_builder.moved.append(
531	                (last_req_index, empty_index,
532	                 MoveDirectionality.UNIDIRECTIONAL))
533	            req_id = self._req_ids[last_req_index]
534	            output_token_ids = self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index]
535	            assert req_id is not None
536	            self._req_ids[empty_index] = req_id
537	            self._req_ids[last_req_index] = None
538	            self.req_output_token_ids[empty_index] = output_token_ids
539	            self.req_output_token_ids[last_req_index] = None
540	            self.req_id_to_index[req_id] = empty_index
541	
542	            num_tokens = self.num_tokens[last_req_index]
543	            self.token_ids_cpu[empty_index, :num_tokens] = self.token_ids_cpu[
544	                last_req_index, :num_tokens]
545	            self.num_tokens[empty_index] = num_tokens
546	            self.num_tokens_no_spec[empty_index] = self.num_tokens_no_spec[
547	                last_req_index]
548	            self.num_prompt_tokens[empty_index] = self.num_prompt_tokens[
549	                last_req_index]
550	            self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[
551	                empty_index] = self.num_computed_tokens_cpu[last_req_index]
552	            self.block_table.move_row(last_req_index, empty_index)
553	            self.temperature_cpu[empty_index] = self.temperature_cpu[
554	                last_req_index]
555	            self.top_p_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_p_cpu[last_req_index]
556	            self.top_k_cpu[empty_index] = self.top_k_cpu[last_req_index]
557	            self.frequency_penalties_cpu[
558	                empty_index] = self.frequency_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
559	            self.presence_penalties_cpu[
560	                empty_index] = self.presence_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
561	            self.repetition_penalties_cpu[
562	                empty_index] = self.repetition_penalties_cpu[last_req_index]
563	            generator = self.generators.pop(last_req_index, None)
564	            if generator is not None:
565	                self.generators[empty_index] = generator
566	
567	            self.request_lora_mapping[empty_index] = self.request_lora_mapping[
568	                last_req_index]
569	
570	            # TODO convert these to LogitsProcessors
571	            if self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor is not None:
572	                self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[
573	                    empty_index] = self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor[
574	                        last_req_index]
575	
576	            bad_words_token_ids = self.bad_words_token_ids.pop(
577	                last_req_index, None)
578	            if bad_words_token_ids is not None:
579	                self.bad_words_token_ids[empty_index] = bad_words_token_ids
580	
581	            # Decrement last_req_index since it is now empty.
582	            last_req_index -= 1
583	
584	        # Trim lists to the batch size.
585	        del self._req_ids[self.num_reqs:]
586	        del self.req_output_token_ids[self.num_reqs:]

588	    def refresh_metadata(self):
589	        """Apply batch updates, reset input batch at end of step
590	        
591	        * Apply batch add/remove/permute to logits procs' states
592	        * If batch state is modified, update sampling metadata
593	        """
594	        batch_update = self.batch_update_builder.get_and_reset(self.num_reqs)
595	        for logit_proc in self.logitsprocs.all:
596	            logit_proc.update_state(batch_update)
597	        if batch_update:
598	            self.sampling_metadata = self._make_sampling_metadata()

600	    def _make_sampling_metadata(self) -> SamplingMetadata:
601	        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
602	        if not self.all_greedy:
603	            temperature = copy_slice(self.temperature_cpu_tensor,
604	                                     self.temperature, num_reqs)
605	        else:
606	            temperature = None
607	        if not self.no_top_p:
608	            copy_slice(self.top_p_cpu_tensor, self.top_p, num_reqs)
609	        if not self.no_top_k:
610	            copy_slice(self.top_k_cpu_tensor, self.top_k, num_reqs)
611	
612	        if not self.no_penalties:
613	            # Since syncing these tensors is expensive only copy them
614	            # if necessary i.e. if there are requests which require
615	            # penalties to be applied during sampling.
616	            copy_slice(self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor,
617	                       self.frequency_penalties, num_reqs)
618	            copy_slice(self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor,
619	                       self.presence_penalties, num_reqs)
620	            copy_slice(self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor,
621	                       self.repetition_penalties, num_reqs)
622	
623	        needs_prompt_token_ids = (not self.no_penalties or
624	                                  (self.num_reqs > 0
625	                                   and self.logits_processing_needs_token_ids))
626	        if needs_prompt_token_ids:
627	            # The prompt tokens are used only for applying penalties or
628	            # step pooling during the sampling/pooling process.
629	            # Hence copy these tensors only when there are requests which
630	            # need penalties/step_pooler to be applied.
631	            prompt_token_ids = self._make_prompt_token_ids_tensor()
632	        else:
633	            prompt_token_ids = None
634	
635	        allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
636	        if not self.no_allowed_token_ids:
637	            assert self.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None
638	            copy_slice(self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor,
639	                       self.allowed_token_ids_mask, num_reqs)
640	            allowed_token_ids_mask = self.allowed_token_ids_mask[:num_reqs]
641	
642	        return SamplingMetadata(
643	            temperature=temperature,
644	            all_greedy=self.all_greedy,
645	            all_random=self.all_random,
646	            top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs],
647	            top_k=None if self.no_top_k else self.top_k[:num_reqs],
648	            generators=self.generators,
649	            max_num_logprobs=self.max_num_logprobs,
650	            prompt_token_ids=prompt_token_ids,
651	            frequency_penalties=self.frequency_penalties[:num_reqs],
652	            presence_penalties=self.presence_penalties[:num_reqs],
653	            repetition_penalties=self.repetition_penalties[:num_reqs],
654	            output_token_ids=cast(list[list[int]], self.req_output_token_ids),
655	            no_penalties=self.no_penalties,
656	            allowed_token_ids_mask=allowed_token_ids_mask,
657	            bad_words_token_ids=self.bad_words_token_ids,
658	            logitsprocs=self.logitsprocs,
659	        )

664	            pooling_params = []

680	    def _make_prompt_token_ids_tensor(self) -> torch.Tensor:
681	        max_prompt_len = self.num_prompt_tokens[:self.num_reqs].max()
682	        prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
683	            (self.num_reqs, max_prompt_len),
684	            device="cpu",
685	            dtype=torch.int64,
686	            pin_memory=self.pin_memory,
687	        )
688	        prompt_token_ids = prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.numpy()
689	        prompt_token_ids[:] = self.token_ids_cpu[:self.
690	                                                 num_reqs, :max_prompt_len]
691	        # Use the value of vocab_size as a pad since we don't have a
692	        # token_id of this value.
693	        for i in range(self.num_reqs):
694	            prompt_token_ids[i, self.num_prompt_tokens[i]:] = self.vocab_size
695	        return prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.to(device=self.device,
696	                                              non_blocking=True)

698	    def make_lora_inputs(
699	        self, num_scheduled_tokens: np.ndarray
700	    ) -> tuple[tuple[int, ...], tuple[int, ...], set[LoRARequest]]:
701	        """
702	        Given the num_scheduled_tokens for each request in the batch, return
703	        datastructures used to activate the current LoRAs.
704	        Returns:
705	            1. prompt_lora_mapping: A tuple of size self.num_reqs where,
706	               prompt_lora_mapping[i] is the LoRA id to use for the ith prompt.
707	            2. token_lora_mapping: A tuple of size np.sum(num_scheduled_tokens)
708	               where, token_lora_mapping[i] is the LoRA id to use for ith token.
709	            3. lora_requests: Set of relevant LoRA requests.
710	        """
711	
712	        req_lora_mapping = self.request_lora_mapping[:self.num_reqs]
713	        prompt_lora_mapping = tuple(req_lora_mapping)
714	        token_lora_mapping = tuple(
715	            req_lora_mapping.repeat(num_scheduled_tokens))
716	        active_lora_requests: set[LoRARequest] = set(
717	            self.lora_id_to_lora_request.values())
718	
719	        return prompt_lora_mapping, token_lora_mapping, active_lora_requests

Excellent! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile the comprehensive report.

## Summary

I've located where the v1 `SamplingMetadata` object is constructed and how its `top_k` and `top_p` tensor fields are built from per-request SamplingParams values. Here are the findings:

### Main Construction Method

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775260877668-fktqcc/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py`

**Method:** `_make_sampling_metadata()` at **lines 600-659**

### How `top_k` and `top_p` Tensors Are Constructed

The construction follows a **two-phase approach**:

#### Phase 1: Per-Request Parameter Storage (in `add_request()` method)

When a request is added to the batch, its sampling params are stored in CPU tensors:

- **Lines 312-320**: Store per-request `top_p` and `top_k` values from `SamplingParams`:
  ```python
  self.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p
  if sampling_params.top_p < 1:
      self.top_p_reqs.add(req_id)
  top_k = sampling_params.top_k
  if 0 < top_k < self.vocab_size:
      self.top_k_reqs.add(req_id)
  else:
      top_k = self.vocab_size
  self.top_k_cpu[req_index] = top_k
  ```

**Key logic**: 
- `top_p_reqs` and `top_k_reqs` are sets that track which requests have non-default values
- If `top_k` is out of range, it's clamped to `vocab_size`

#### Phase 2: SamplingMetadata Construction (in `_make_sampling_metadata()` method)

The method builds GPU tensors conditionally:

- **Lines 607-610**: Copy CPU tensors to GPU only if needed:
  ```python
  if not self.no_top_p:
      copy_slice(self.top_p_cpu_tensor, self.top_p, num_reqs)
  if not self.no_top_k:
      copy_slice(self.top_k_cpu_tensor, self.top_k, num_reqs)
  ```

- **Lines 646-647**: Construct the actual tensors for SamplingMetadata:
  ```python
  top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs],
  top_k=None if self.no_top_k else self.top_k[:num_reqs],
  ```

### Conditional Logic (Lines 734-739)

The properties that control whether tensors are included:

```python
@property
def no_top_p(self) -> bool:
    return len(self.top_p_reqs) == 0

@property
def no_top_k(self) -> bool:
    return len(self.top_k_reqs) == 0
```

**Pattern**: If all requests have `top_p >= 1.0` or `top_k >= vocab_size` (i.e., disabled), the corresponding property returns `True`, and the field is set to `None` in SamplingMetadata instead of including a tensor.

### Complete SamplingMetadata Constructor Call

**Lines 642-659**:
```python
return SamplingMetadata(
    temperature=temperature,
    all_greedy=self.all_greedy,
    all_random=self.all_random,
    top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs],
    top_k=None if self.no_top_k else self.top_k[:num_reqs],
    generators=self.generators,
    max_num_logprobs=self.max_num_logprobs,
    prompt_token_ids=prompt_token_ids,
    frequency_penalties=self.frequency_penalties[:num_reqs],
    presence_penalties=self.presence_penalties[:num_reqs],
    repetition_penalties=self.repetition_penalties[:num_reqs],
    output_token_ids=cast(list[list[int]], self.req_output_token_ids),
    no_penalties=self.no_penalties,
    allowed_token_ids_mask=allowed_token_ids_mask,
    bad_words_token_ids=self.bad_words_token_ids,
    logitsprocs=self.logitsprocs,
)
```

### Key Points

1. **No explicit iteration over requests in construction** - the tensors are pre-built as part of the InputBatch state management
2. **Slicing to active batch size** - both `top_p` and `top_k` are sliced to `[:num_reqs]` to match the current batch size
3. **Optimization**: When all requests have sampling disabled for a parameter, the field is set to `None` instead of creating an unnecessary tensor
4. **Update trigger**: The metadata is regenerated via `refresh_metadata()` (line 598) whenever batch state changes

      copy_slice(self.top_k_cpu_tensor, self.top_k, num_reqs)
  ```

- **Lines 646-647**: Construct the actual tensors for SamplingMetadata:
  ```python
  top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs],
  top_k=None if self.no_top_k else self.top_k[:num_reqs],
  ```

### Conditional Logic (Lines 734-739)

The properties that control whether tensors are included:

```python
@property
def no_top_p(self) -> bool:
    return len(self.top_p_reqs) == 0

@property
def no_top_k(self) -> bool:
    return len(self.top_k_reqs) == 0
```

**Pattern**: If all requests have `top_p >= 1.0` or `top_k >= vocab_size` (i.e., disabled), the corresponding property returns `True`, and the field is set to `None` in SamplingMetadata instead of including a tensor.

### Complete SamplingMetadata Constructor Call

**Lines 642-659**:
```python
return SamplingMetadata(
    temperature=temperature,
    all_greedy=self.all_greedy,
    all_random=self.all_random,
    top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs],
    top_k=None if self.no_top_k else self.top_k[:num_reqs],
    generators=self.generators,
    max_num_logprobs=self.max_num_logprobs,
    prompt_token_ids=prompt_token_ids,
    frequency_penalties=self.frequency_penalties[:num_reqs],
    presence_penalties=self.presence_penalties[:num_reqs],
    repetition_penalties=self.repetition_penalties[:num_reqs],
    output_token_ids=cast(list[list[int]], self.req_output_token_ids),
    no_penalties=self.no_penalties,
    allowed_token_ids_mask=allowed_token_ids_mask,
    bad_words_token_ids=self.bad_words_token_ids,
    logitsprocs=self.logitsprocs,
)
```

### Key Points

1. **No explicit iteration over requests in construction** - the tensors are pre-built as part of the InputBatch state management
2. **Slicing to active batch size** - both `top_p` and `top_k` are sliced to `[:num_reqs]` to match the current batch size
3. **Optimization**: When all requests have sampling disabled for a parameter, the field is set to `None` instead of creating an unnecessary tensor
4. **Update trigger**: The metadata is regenerated via `refresh_metadata()` (line 598) whenever batch state changes

# Plan: Add Top-A Sampling to vLLM

## Context

Top-A sampling keeps only tokens whose probability is at least `a * max_probability`, then renormalizes. It is mathematically identical to `min_p` (both threshold relative to the max-prob token), but is a distinct, separately named parameter used in other inference frameworks. Adding it follows the same threading pattern already established for `min_p`.

`top_a = 0.0` (default) means disabled (no-op). Valid range: `[0.0, 1.0]`.

---

## Critical Files to Modify

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `vllm/sampling_params.py` | Add field, docstring, validation, `__repr__` |
| `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | Add `top_as` tensor to `SamplingTensors`; thread through `from_sampling_metadata()` / `from_lists()` |
| `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | Add `_apply_top_a()` function; call it in `forward()` |
| `vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` | Add `top_a: Optional[torch.Tensor]` field |
| `vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py` | Add `top_a` CPU array, GPU tensor, `top_a_reqs` set; build tensor in `_make_sampling_metadata()` |
| `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` | Apply `apply_top_a()` before TopKTopPSampler call |
| `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` | Add `apply_top_a()` helper function |

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — `vllm/sampling_params.py`

**1a. Add field declaration** (after `min_p: float = 0.0` ~line 215):
```python
top_a: float = 0.0
```

**1b. Add docstring entry** in the class docstring (after `min_p` entry, ~line 153):
```
top_a: Float that controls Top-A sampling. Keeps only tokens whose
    probability is at least ``top_a * max_probability``, then
    renormalizes. Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0.0 to disable (default).
```

**1c. Add validation** in `_verify_args()` (after `min_p` validation ~line 434):
```python
if not 0.0 <= self.top_a <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError("top_a must be in [0, 1], got "
                     f"{self.top_a}.")
```

**1d. Reset in greedy path** in `__post_init__()` (after `self.min_p = 0.0` ~line 380):
```python
self.top_a = 0.0
```

**1e. Add to `__repr__`** (after `min_p=...` entry ~line 573):
```python
f"top_a={self.top_a}, "
```

---

### Step 2 — `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (v0 path)

**2a. Add field to `SamplingTensors` dataclass** (after `min_ps: torch.Tensor` ~line 379):
```python
top_as: torch.Tensor
```

**2b. In `from_sampling_metadata()`** (lines 386–495):

- Add list init at top with other lists (~line 399):
  ```python
  top_as: list[float] = []
  do_top_a = False
  ```

- Extract value per seq_group (after `min_p = sampling_params.min_p` ~line 416):
  ```python
  top_a = sampling_params.top_a
  ```

- Add `do_top_a` flag check (after `do_min_p` check ~line 430):
  ```python
  if not do_top_a and top_a > _SAMPLING_EPS:
      do_top_a = True
  ```

- Populate list in prompt_logprobs block (after `min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len` ~line 446):
  ```python
  top_as += [top_a] * prefill_len
  ```

- Populate list in `do_sample` block (after `min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens` ~line 457):
  ```python
  top_as += [top_a] * sample_lens
  ```

- Pass to `from_lists()` (add `top_as` after `top_ks` argument ~line 484):
  ```python
  top_as,
  ```

- Update return tuple from 4-tuple to 5-tuple (line 495):
  ```python
  return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p, do_top_a)
  ```

**2c. In `from_lists()` signature** (add parameter after `top_ks: list[int]` ~line 502):
```python
top_as: list[float],
```

Add tensor construction (after `min_ps_t` creation ~line 556):
```python
top_as_t = torch.tensor(
    top_as,
    device="cpu",
    dtype=dtype,
    pin_memory=pin_memory,
)
```

Add to `cls(...)` constructor call (after `min_ps=...` ~line 587):
```python
top_as=top_as_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
```

---

### Step 3 — `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (v0 path)

**3a. Find `_init_sampling_tensors()`** — it calls `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` and unpacks the 4-tuple. Update the unpack to 5-tuple:
```python
(self._sampling_tensors,
 self._do_penalties,
 self._do_top_p_top_k,
 self._do_min_p,
 self._do_top_a) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(...)
```

**3b. In `forward()` method** — add instance variable extraction (after `do_min_p = self._do_min_p` ~line 267):
```python
do_top_a = self._do_top_a
```

Add the filter call (after the `_apply_min_p` call ~line 289):
```python
if do_top_a:
    logits = _apply_top_a(logits, sampling_tensors.top_as)
```

**3c. Add `_apply_top_a()` function** (after `_apply_min_p()` ~line 441):
```python
def _apply_top_a(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    top_a: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply Top-A sampling: keep tokens where p >= top_a * max_p."""
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_top_a
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

*(This mirrors `_apply_min_p()` exactly.)*

---

### Step 4 — `vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py`

Add field after `top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]` (line 20):
```python
top_a: Optional[torch.Tensor]
```

---

### Step 5 — `vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py`

This file manages pre-allocated CPU/GPU tensor storage for all batch parameters.

**5a. In `__init__()` / class initialization**, alongside `top_p_cpu`, `top_k_cpu`:
```python
self.top_a_cpu = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.float32)
self.top_a_reqs: set[str] = set()
```
And the pre-allocated GPU tensor (matching pattern of `top_p` / `top_k`):
```python
self.top_a = torch.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
self.top_a_cpu_tensor = torch.from_numpy(self.top_a_cpu).pin_memory()
```

**5b. In `add_request()`**, after `top_k` storage logic (~line 320):
```python
top_a = sampling_params.top_a
if top_a > 0.0:
    self.top_a_reqs.add(req_id)
self.top_a_cpu[req_index] = top_a
```

**5c. In `remove_request()`**, alongside `top_p_reqs` / `top_k_reqs` cleanup:
```python
self.top_a_reqs.discard(req_id)
```

**5d. Add `no_top_a` property** (alongside `no_top_p` / `no_top_k`):
```python
@property
def no_top_a(self) -> bool:
    return len(self.top_a_reqs) == 0
```

**5e. In `_make_sampling_metadata()`**, add CPU→GPU copy (alongside `top_p` / `top_k`):
```python
if not self.no_top_a:
    copy_slice(self.top_a_cpu_tensor, self.top_a, num_reqs)
```

Add to the `SamplingMetadata(...)` constructor call (after `top_k=...`):
```python
top_a=None if self.no_top_a else self.top_a[:num_reqs],
```

---

### Step 6 — `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

Add a standalone helper function (e.g., after `apply_top_k_only()` ~line 218):
```python
def apply_top_a(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    a: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply Top-A sampling: keep tokens where p >= a * max_p.

    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_a = a.unsqueeze(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_a
    logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

---

### Step 7 — `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py`

**7a. Import** `apply_top_a` at the top (alongside other imports from `topk_topp_sampler`):
```python
from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import apply_top_a
```

**7b. Apply the filter** before the `self.topk_topp_sampler(...)` call (~line 121):
```python
if sampling_metadata.top_a is not None:
    logits = apply_top_a(logits, sampling_metadata.top_a)
```

---

## Execution Order / Dependencies

```
Step 1 (sampling_params.py)     ← no dependencies
Step 2 (sampling_metadata.py)   ← depends on Step 1 (reads top_a from params)
Step 3 (sampler.py v0)          ← depends on Step 2 (uses SamplingTensors.top_as)
Step 4 (v1/metadata.py)         ← no dependencies
Step 5 (gpu_input_batch.py)     ← depends on Steps 1 & 4
Step 6 (topk_topp_sampler.py)   ← no dependencies
Step 7 (v1/sampler.py)          ← depends on Steps 4, 5, 6
```

---

## Verification

Since we cannot run vLLM, verify correctness by:

1. **Static checks**: `python -m py_compile vllm/sampling_params.py` — confirms no syntax errors.
2. **Field presence**: `grep -n "top_a" vllm/sampling_params.py vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` — confirms the parameter is threaded throughout.
3. **Return arity**: Search for callers of `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata` to confirm 5-tuple unpack is consistent.
4. **Logic review**: The `_apply_top_a` implementation can be validated by inspection against `_apply_min_p` — they are structurally identical.
5. **Test import**: `python -c "from vllm.sampling_params import SamplingParams; p = SamplingParams(top_a=0.5); print(p.top_a)"` — if vLLM is installable.

# Plan: Add Top-A Sampling to vLLM

## Context

Top-A sampling keeps only tokens whose probability is at least `a * max_probability`, then renormalizes. It is mathematically identical to `min_p` (both threshold relative to the max-prob token), but is a distinct, separately named parameter used in other inference frameworks. Adding it follows the same threading pattern already established for `min_p`.

`top_a = 0.0` (default) means disabled (no-op). Valid range: `[0.0, 1.0]`.

---

## Critical Files to Modify

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `vllm/sampling_params.py` | Add field, docstring, validation, `__repr__` |
| `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | Add `top_as` tensor to `SamplingTensors`; thread through `from_sampling_metadata()` / `from_lists()` |
| `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | Add `_apply_top_a()` function; call it in `forward()` |
| `vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` | Add `top_a: Optional[torch.Tensor]` field |
| `vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py` | Add `top_a` CPU array, GPU tensor, `top_a_reqs` set; build tensor in `_make_sampling_metadata()` |
| `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` | Apply `apply_top_a()` before TopKTopPSampler call |
| `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` | Add `apply_top_a()` helper function |

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

**1b. Add docstring entry** in the class docstring (after `min_p` entry, ~line 153):
```
top_a: Float that controls Top-A sampling. Keeps only tokens whose
    probability is at least ``top_a * max_probability``, then
    renormalizes. Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0.0 to disable (default).
```

**1c. Add validation** in `_verify_args()` (after `min_p` validation ~line 434):
```python
if not 0.0 <= self.top_a <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError("top_a must be in [0, 1], got "
                     f"{self.top_a}.")
```

**1d. Reset in greedy path** in `__post_init__()` (after `self.min_p = 0.0` ~line 380):
```python
self.top_a = 0.0
```

**1e. Add to `__repr__`** (after `min_p=...` entry ~line 573):
```python
f"top_a={self.top_a}, "
```

---

### Step 2 — `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (v0 path)

**2a. Add field to `SamplingTensors` dataclass** (after `min_ps: torch.Tensor` ~line 379):
```python

```

  ```

  ```

  ```

- Update return tuple from 4-tuple to 5-tuple (line 495):
  ```python
  return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p, do_top_a)
  ```

**2c. In `from_lists()` signature** (add parameter after `top_ks: list[int]` ~line 502):
```python
top_as: list[float],
```

Add tensor construction (after `min_ps_t` creation ~line 556):

    top_as,
    device="cpu",
    dtype=dtype,
    pin_memory=pin_memory,
)
```

Add to `cls(...)` constructor call (after `min_ps=...` ~line 587):

```

---

### Step 3 — `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (v0 path)

**3a. Find `_init_sampling_tensors()`** — it calls `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()` and unpacks the 4-tuple. Update the unpack to 5-tuple:
```python
(self._sampling_tensors,
 self._do_penalties,
 self._do_top_p_top_k,
 self._do_min_p,
 self._do_top_a) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(...)
```

**3b. In `forward()` method** — add instance variable extraction (after `do_min_p = self._do_min_p` ~line 267):
```python
do_top_a = self._do_top_a
```

Add the filter call (after the `_apply_min_p` call ~line 289):
```python
if do_top_a:
    logits = _apply_top_a(logits, sampling_tensors.top_as)
```

**3c. Add `_apply_top_a()` function** (after `_apply_min_p()` ~line 441):
```python
def _apply_top_a(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    top_a: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply Top-A sampling: keep tokens where p >= top_a * max_p."""
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_top_a
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

*(This mirrors `_apply_min_p()` exactly.)*

**5a. In `__init__()` / class initialization**, alongside `top_p_cpu`, `top_k_cpu`:

And the pre-allocated GPU tensor (matching pattern of `top_p` / `top_k`):

self.top_a_cpu_tensor = torch.from_numpy(self.top_a_cpu).pin_memory()
```

**5b. In `add_request()`**, after `top_k` storage logic (~line 320):

top_a = sampling_params.top_a
if top_a > 0.0:
    self.top_a_reqs.add(req_id)
self.top_a_cpu[req_index] = top_a
```

**5c. In `remove_request()`**, alongside `top_p_reqs` / `top_k_reqs` cleanup:
```python
self.top_a_reqs.discard(req_id)
```

**5d. Add `no_top_a` property** (alongside `no_top_p` / `no_top_k`):
```python
@property
def no_top_a(self) -> bool:
    return len(self.top_a_reqs) == 0
```

**5e. In `_make_sampling_metadata()`**, add CPU→GPU copy (alongside `top_p` / `top_k`):
```python
if not self.no_top_a:
    copy_slice(self.top_a_cpu_tensor, self.top_a, num_reqs)
```

Add to the `SamplingMetadata(...)` constructor call (after `top_k=...`):
```python
top_a=None if self.no_top_a else self.top_a[:num_reqs],
```

---

### Step 6 — `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

Add a standalone helper function (e.g., after `apply_top_k_only()` ~line 218):

def apply_top_a(

) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply Top-A sampling: keep tokens where p >= a * max_p.

    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.

---

```python
self.top_a_cpu = np.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=np.float32)
self.top_a_reqs: set[str] = set()
```
And the pre-allocated GPU tensor (matching pattern of `top_p` / `top_k`):
```python
self.top_a = torch.zeros(max_num_reqs, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
self.top_a_cpu_tensor = torch.from_numpy(self.top_a_cpu).pin_memory()
```

**5b. In `add_request()`**, after `top_k` storage logic (~line 320):
```python
top_a = sampling_params.top_a
if top_a > 0.0:
    self.top_a_reqs.add(req_id)
self.top_a_cpu[req_index] = top_a
```

**5c. In `remove_request()`**, alongside `top_p_reqs` / `top_k_reqs` cleanup:
```python
self.top_a_reqs.discard(req_id)
```

**5d. Add `no_top_a` property** (alongside `no_top_p` / `no_top_k`):
```python
@property
def no_top_a(self) -> bool:
    return len(self.top_a_reqs) == 0
```

**5e. In `_make_sampling_metadata()`**, add CPU→GPU copy (alongside `top_p` / `top_k`):
```python
if not self.no_top_a:
    copy_slice(self.top_a_cpu_tensor, self.top_a, num_reqs)
```

Add to the `SamplingMetadata(...)` constructor call (after `top_k=...`):
```python
top_a=None if self.no_top_a else self.top_a[:num_reqs],
```

---

### Step 6 — `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

Add a standalone helper function (e.g., after `apply_top_k_only()` ~line 218):
```python
def apply_top_a(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    a: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply Top-A sampling: keep tokens where p >= a * max_p.

    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_a = a.unsqueeze(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_a
    logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

---

### Step 7 — `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py`

**7a. Import** `apply_top_a` at the top (alongside other imports from `topk_topp_sampler`):
```python
from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import apply_top_a
```

**7b. Apply the filter** before the `self.topk_topp_sampler(...)` call (~line 121):
```python
if sampling_metadata.top_a is not None:
    logits = apply_top_a(logits, sampling_metadata.top_a)
```

---

## Execution Order / Dependencies

```
Step 1 (sampling_params.py)     ← no dependencies
Step 2 (sampling_metadata.py)   ← depends on Step 1 (reads top_a from params)
Step 3 (sampler.py v0)          ← depends on Step 2 (uses SamplingTensors.top_as)
Step 4 (v1/metadata.py)         ← no dependencies
Step 5 (gpu_input_batch.py)     ← depends on Steps 1 & 4
Step 6 (topk_topp_sampler.py)   ← no dependencies
Step 7 (v1/sampler.py)          ← depends on Steps 4, 5, 6
```

---

## Verification

Since we cannot run vLLM, verify correctness by:

1. **Static checks**: `python -m py_compile vllm/sampling_params.py` — confirms no syntax errors.
2. **Field presence**: `grep -n "top_a" vllm/sampling_params.py vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` — confirms the parameter is threaded throughout.
3. **Return arity**: Search for callers of `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata` to confirm 5-tuple unpack is consistent.
4. **Logic review**: The `_apply_top_a` implementation can be validated by inspection against `_apply_min_p` — they are structurally identical.
5. **Test import**: `python -c "from vllm.sampling_params import SamplingParams; p = SamplingParams(top_a=0.5); print(p.top_a)"` — if vLLM is installable.

file.content:
Here's a summary of the plan I've written to `/Users/timpietrusky/.claude/plans/greedy-mixing-cerf.md`:

---

## What the plan does

Adds `top_a` (Top-A sampling) throughout vLLM, following the exact same pattern as `min_p` (which is structurally identical — both threshold tokens relative to `max_probability`).

## 7 files to touch, in dependency order

| # | File | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `vllm/sampling_params.py` | Add `top_a: float = 0.0` field, docstring entry, `[0,1]` validation, greedy reset, `__repr__` entry |
| 2 | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | Add `top_as` tensor to `SamplingTensors`; thread `top_a` through `from_sampling_metadata()` (adds `do_top_a` flag, returns 5-tuple) and `from_lists()` |
| 3 | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | Add `_apply_top_a()` function (mirrors `_apply_min_p` exactly); call it in `forward()` when `do_top_a` is true |
| 4 | `vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` | Add `top_a: Optional[torch.Tensor]` field |
| 5 | `vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py` | Add CPU array, GPU tensor, `top_a_reqs` set, `no_top_a` property; build tensor in `_make_sampling_metadata()` |
| 6 | `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` | Add `apply_top_a()` helper (softmax → find max_p → mask tokens below `a * max_p`) |
| 7 | `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` | Import `apply_top_a`; apply it to logits before calling `TopKTopPSampler` when `top_a is not None` |

## The core filter logic (same in both v0 and v1)

```python
probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
scaled_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze(dim=1) * top_probs  # threshold per batch item
logits.masked_fill_(probs < scaled_top_a, -float("inf"))
```

This is applied **after** temperature scaling and top-k/top-p but **before** the final softmax that produces sampling probabilities.

3	"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""

31	@dataclass
32	class GuidedDecodingParams:
33	    """One of these fields will be used to build a logit processor."""
34	    json: Optional[Union[str, dict]] = None
35	    regex: Optional[str] = None
36	    choice: Optional[list[str]] = None
37	    grammar: Optional[str] = None
38	    json_object: Optional[bool] = None
39	    """These are other options that can be set"""
40	    backend: Optional[str] = None
41	    backend_was_auto: bool = False
42	    disable_fallback: bool = False
43	    disable_any_whitespace: bool = False
44	    disable_additional_properties: bool = False
45	    whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
46	    structural_tag: Optional[str] = None
47	
48	    @staticmethod
49	    def from_optional(
50	        json: Optional[Union[dict, BaseModel, str]] = None,
51	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
52	        choice: Optional[list[str]] = None,
53	        grammar: Optional[str] = None,
54	        json_object: Optional[bool] = None,
55	        backend: Optional[str] = None,
56	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
57	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
58	    ) -> Optional["GuidedDecodingParams"]:
59	        if all(arg is None for arg in (json, regex, choice, grammar,
60	                                       json_object, structural_tag)):
61	            return None
62	        # Extract json schemas from pydantic models
63	        if isinstance(json, (BaseModel, type(BaseModel))):
64	            json = json.model_json_schema()
65	        return GuidedDecodingParams(
66	            json=json,
67	            regex=regex,
68	            choice=choice,
69	            grammar=grammar,
70	            json_object=json_object,
71	            backend=backend,
72	            whitespace_pattern=whitespace_pattern,
73	            structural_tag=structural_tag,
74	        )
75	
76	    def __post_init__(self):
77	        """Validate that some fields are mutually exclusive."""
78	        guide_count = sum([
79	            self.json is not None, self.regex is not None, self.choice
80	            is not None, self.grammar is not None, self.json_object is not None
81	        ])
82	        if guide_count > 1:
83	            raise ValueError(
84	                "You can only use one kind of guided decoding but multiple are "
85	                f"specified: {self.__dict__}")
86	
87	        if self.backend is not None and ":" in self.backend:
88	            self._extract_backend_options()
89	
90	    @deprecated(
91	        "Passing guided decoding backend options inside backend in the format "
92	        "'backend:...' is deprecated. This will be removed in v0.10.0. Please "
93	        "use the dedicated arguments '--disable-fallback', "
94	        "'--disable-any-whitespace' and '--disable-additional-properties' "
95	        "instead.")
96	    def _extract_backend_options(self):
97	        """Extract backend options from the backend string."""
98	        assert isinstance(self.backend, str)
99	        self.backend, options = self.backend.split(":")
100	        options_set = set(options.strip().split(","))
101	        if "no-fallback" in options_set:
102	            self.disable_fallback = True
103	        if "disable-any-whitespace" in options_set:
104	            self.disable_any_whitespace = True
105	        if "no-additional-properties" in options_set:
106	            self.disable_additional_properties = True
107	
108	
109	class RequestOutputKind(Enum):
110	    # Return entire output so far in every RequestOutput
111	    CUMULATIVE = 0
112	    # Return only deltas in each RequestOutput
113	    DELTA = 1
114	    # Do not return intermediate RequestOutput
115	    FINAL_ONLY = 2
116	
117	
118	class SamplingParams(
119	        msgspec.Struct,
120	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
121	        # required for @cached_property.
122	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
123	    """Sampling parameters for text generation.
124	
125	    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
126	    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
127	    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
131	        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
132	            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
133	            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
134	            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
135	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
136	            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
137	            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
138	            tokens.
139	        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
140	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
141	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
142	            repeat tokens.
143	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
144	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
145	            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
146	            the model to repeat tokens.
147	        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
148	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
149	            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
150	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
151	            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
152	        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
153	            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
154	        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
155	            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
156	            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
157	        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
158	        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
159	            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
160	        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
161	            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
162	            the stop tokens are special tokens.
163	        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
164	            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
165	            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
166	            can complete the sequence.
167	        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
168	            output text. Defaults to False.
169	        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
170	            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
171	        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
172	        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
173	            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
174	        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
175	            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
176	            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
177	            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
178	            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
179	            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
180	            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
181	        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
182	        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
183	        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
184	        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
185	            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
186	        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
187	            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
188	            a first argument.
189	        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
190	            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
191	            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
192	            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
193	        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
194	            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
195	        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
196	            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
197	        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
198	            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
199	            Defaults to None.
200	        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
201	            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
202	            sampling implementations.
203	    """
204	
205	    n: int = 1
206	    best_of: Optional[int] = None
207	    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
208	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
209	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0

211	    temperature: float = 1.0
